heading: Special rules in case of foreign oil and gas income

L. 113–295December 31, 2008December 31, 2008“(i) by substituting ‘oil and gas extraction taxes’ for ‘foreign oil and gas taxes’ each place it appears in paragraphs (1), (2), and (3), and“(ii) by computing, for purposes of paragraph (2)(A), the limitation under subparagraph (A) for the year to which such taxes are carried by substituting ‘foreign oil and gas extraction income’ for ‘foreign oil and gas income’ in subsection (a).”2008—Subsecs. (a), (b). , amended subsecs. (a) and (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) related to reduction in amount of oil and gas extraction taxes paid or accrued for purposes of section 901 and subsec. (f)(3)(C). , struck out subpar. (A) substituted “the processing of minerals extracted (by the taxpayer or by any other person) from oil or gas wells into their primary products” for “the extraction (by the taxpayer or any other person) of minerals from oil or gas wells”, deleted subpar. (B) which had provided that foreign oil related income meant the taxable income derived from sources outside the United States and its possessions from the processing of minerals from oil or gas wells into their primary products, redesignated subpar. (c)(4). , substituted provisions regarding the recapture of foreign oil and gas extraction losses by recharacterization of later extraction income for provisions that if, for any foreign country for any taxable year, the taxpayer would have had a net operating loss if only items from sources within such country (including deductions properly apportioned or allocated thereto) which related to the extraction of minerals from oil or gas wells had been taken into account, such items would not be taken into account in computing foreign oil and gas extraction income for such year, but would be taken into account in computing foreign oil related income for such year.Pub. Effective Date of 1982 Amendment, , , as amended by , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–248, title II, § 211(e)96 Stat. 450Pub. L. 97–448, title III, § 306(a)(5)96 Stat. 2401Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 712(e)98 Stat. 947Sept. 3, 1982July 18, 1984“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [amending this section and ] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .section 904 of this titleDecember 31, 1982“(2)(b)(f)Retention of old sections 907and 904(4) where taxpayer had separate basket foreign loss.—“(A)In general.—If, after applying old sections 907(b) and 904(f)(4) to a taxable year beginning before , the taxpayer had a separate basket foreign loss, such loss shall not be recaptured from income of a kind not taken into account in computing the amount of such separate basket foreign loss more rapidly than ratably over the 8-year period (or such shorter period as the taxpayer may select) beginning with the first taxable year beginning after .January 1, 1983December 31, 1982“(B)Definitions.—For purposes of this paragraph—“(i)The term ‘separate basket foreign loss’ means any foreign loss attributable to activities taken into account (or not taken into account) in determining foreign oil related income (as defined in old section 907(c)(2)).“(ii)An ‘old’ section is such section as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Sept. 3, 1982

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Nov. 6, 1978Oct. 22, 1986“(i)The amendments made by this paragraph [amending this section and ] shall apply, in the case of individuals, to taxable years ending after , and, in the case of corporations, to taxable years ending after .section 904 of this titleDecember 31, 1974December 31, 1976“(ii)In the case of any taxable year ending after , with respect to foreign oil related income (within the meaning of section 907(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954]), the overall limitation provided by section 904(a)(2) of such Code shall apply and the per-country limitation provided by section 904(a)(1) of such Code shall not apply.”December 31, 1975

Tax Credit for Production-Sharing Contracts, , , as amended by , §§ 701(u)(9), 703(h)(1), , , 2940; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–455, title X, § 1035(c)90 Stat. 1631Pub. L. 95–600, title VII92 Stat. 2916Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Oct. 4, 1976Nov. 6, 1978Oct. 22, 1986“(1)For purposes of section 901 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], there shall be treated as income, war profits, and excess profits taxes to be taken into account under section 907(a) of such Code amounts designated as income taxes of a foreign government by such government (which otherwise would not be treated as taxes for purposes of section 901 of such Code) with respect to production-sharing contracts for the extraction of foreign oil or gas.“(2)The amounts specified in paragraph (1) shall not exceed the lessor of—“(A)the product of the foreign oil and gas extraction income (as defined in section 907(c) of such Code) with respect to all such production-sharing contracts multiplied by the sum of the normal tax rate and the surtax rate for the taxable year specified in section 11 of such Code, or“(B)the excess of the total amount of foreign oil and gas extraction income (as so defined) for the taxable year multiplied by the sum of the normal tax rate and the surtax rate for the taxable year specified in section 11 of such Code over the amount of any income, war profits, and excess profits taxes paid or accrued (or deemed to have been paid) without regard to paragraph (1) during the taxable year with respect to foreign oil and gas extraction income.“(3)The production-sharing contracts taken into account for purposes of paragraph (1) shall be those contracts which were entered into before , for the sharing of foreign oil and gas production with a foreign government (or an entity owned by such government) with respect to which amounts claimed as taxes paid or accrued to such foreign government for taxable years beginning before , will not be disallowed as taxes. A contract described in the preceding sentence shall be taken into account under paragraph (1) only with respect to amounts (A) paid or accrued to the foreign government before , and (B) attributable to income earned before such date.”April 8, 1976June 30, 1976January 1, 1978

A, title VII, § 712(e)98 Stat. 947Sept. 3, 1982July 18, 1984“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [amending this section and ] shall apply to taxable years beginning after .section 904 of this titleDecember 31, 1982“(2)(b)(f)Retention of old sections 907and 904(4) where taxpayer had separate basket foreign loss.—“(A)In general.—If, after applying old sections 907(b) and 904(f)(4) to a taxable year beginning before , the taxpayer had a separate basket foreign loss, such loss shall not be recaptured from income of a kind not taken into account in computing the amount of such separate basket foreign loss more rapidly than ratably over the 8-year period (or such shorter period as the taxpayer may select) beginning with the first taxable year beginning after .January 1, 1983December 31, 1982“(B)Definitions.—For purposes of this paragraph—“(i)The term ‘separate basket foreign loss’ means any foreign loss attributable to activities taken into account (or not taken into account) in determining foreign oil related income (as defined in old section 907(c)(2)).“(ii)An ‘old’ section is such section as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Sept. 3, 1982

Tax Credit for Production-Sharing Contracts, , , as amended by , §§ 701(u)(9), 703(h)(1), , , 2940; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 94–455, title X, § 1035(c)90 Stat. 1631Pub. L. 95–600, title VII92 Stat. 2916Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Oct. 4, 1976Nov. 6, 1978Oct. 22, 1986“(1)For purposes of section 901 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], there shall be treated as income, war profits, and excess profits taxes to be taken into account under section 907(a) of such Code amounts designated as income taxes of a foreign government by such government (which otherwise would not be treated as taxes for purposes of section 901 of such Code) with respect to production-sharing contracts for the extraction of foreign oil or gas.“(2)The amounts specified in paragraph (1) shall not exceed the lessor of—“(A)the product of the foreign oil and gas extraction income (as defined in section 907(c) of such Code) with respect to all such production-sharing contracts multiplied by the sum of the normal tax rate and the surtax rate for the taxable year specified in section 11 of such Code, or“(B)the excess of the total amount of foreign oil and gas extraction income (as so defined) for the taxable year multiplied by the sum of the normal tax rate and the surtax rate for the taxable year specified in section 11 of such Code over the amount of any income, war profits, and excess profits taxes paid or accrued (or deemed to have been paid) without regard to paragraph (1) during the taxable year with respect to foreign oil and gas extraction income.“(3)The production-sharing contracts taken into account for purposes of paragraph (1) shall be those contracts which were entered into before , for the sharing of foreign oil and gas production with a foreign government (or an entity owned by such government) with respect to which amounts claimed as taxes paid or accrued to such foreign government for taxable years beginning before , will not be disallowed as taxes. A contract described in the preceding sentence shall be taken into account under paragraph (1) only with respect to amounts (A) paid or accrued to the foreign government before , and (B) attributable to income earned before such date.”April 8, 1976June 30, 1976January 1, 1978

children.heading: Reduction in amount allowed as foreign tax under section 901
children.label: a Reduction in amount allowed as foreign tax under section 901

children.text: the amount of the combined foreign oil and gas income for the taxable year,

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a corporation, the percentage which is equal to the highest rate of tax specified under section 11(b), or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an individual, a fraction the numerator of which is the tax against which the credit under section 901(a) is taken and the denominator of which is the taxpayer’s entire taxable income.

children.heading: Combined foreign oil and gas income; foreign oil and gas taxes
children.label: b Combined foreign oil and gas income; foreign oil and gas taxes

children.heading: Combined foreign oil and gas income
children.label: 1 Combined foreign oil and gas income
A
children.text: foreign oil and gas extraction income, and

children.label: B
children.text: foreign oil related income.

children.heading: Foreign oil and gas taxes
children.label: 2 Foreign oil and gas taxes
A
children.text: oil and gas extraction taxes, and

children.label: B
children.text: any income, war profits, and excess profits taxes paid or accrued (or deemed to have been paid or accrued under section 960) during the taxable year with respect to foreign oil related income (determined without regard to subsection (c)(4)) or loss which would be taken into account for purposes of section 901 without regard to this section.

children.heading: Foreign income definitions and special rules
children.label: c Foreign income definitions and special rules

children.heading: Foreign oil and gas extraction income
children.label: 1 Foreign oil and gas extraction income
A
children.text: the extraction (by the taxpayer or any other person) of minerals from oil or gas wells, or

children.label: B
children.text: the sale or exchange of assets used by the taxpayer in the trade or business described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Foreign oil related income
children.label: 2 Foreign oil related income
A
children.text: the processing of minerals extracted (by the taxpayer or by any other person) from oil or gas wells into their primary products,

children.label: B
children.text: the transportation of such minerals or primary products,

children.label: C
children.text: the distribution or sale of such minerals or primary products,

children.label: D
children.text: the disposition of assets used by the taxpayer in the trade or business described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C), or

children.label: E
children.text: the performance of any other related service.

children.heading: Dividends, interest, partnership distribution, etc.
children.label: 3 Dividends, interest, partnership distribution, etc.
A
children.text: interest, to the extent the category of income of such interest is determined under section 904(d)(3),

children.label: B
children.text: amounts with respect to which taxes are deemed paid under section 960, and

children.label: C
children.text: the taxpayer’s distributive share of the income of partnerships,

children.heading: Recapture of foreign oil and gas losses by recharacterizing later combined foreign oil and gas income
children.label: 4 Recapture of foreign oil and gas losses by recharacterizing later combined foreign oil and gas income

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
i
children.text: first by the amount determined under subparagraph (B), and

children.label: ii
children.text: then by the amount determined under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Reduction for pre-2009 foreign oil extraction losses
children.label: B Reduction for pre-2009 foreign oil extraction losses
i
children.text: the foreign oil and gas extraction income of the taxpayer for the taxable year (determined without regard to this paragraph), or

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate amount of foreign oil extraction losses for preceding taxable years beginning after , and before , overDecember 31, 1982January 1, 2009

children.label: II
children.text: so much of such aggregate amount as was recharacterized under this paragraph (as in effect before and after the date of the enactment of the Energy Improvement and Extension Act of 2008) for preceding taxable years beginning after .December 31, 1982

children.heading: Reduction for post-2008 foreign oil and gas losses
children.label: C Reduction for post-2008 foreign oil and gas losses
i
children.text: the combined foreign oil and gas income of the taxpayer for the taxable year (determined without regard to this paragraph), reduced by an amount equal to the reduction under subparagraph (A) for the taxable year, or

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate amount of foreign oil and gas losses for preceding taxable years beginning after , overDecember 31, 2008

children.label: II
children.text: so much of such aggregate amount as was recharacterized under this paragraph for preceding taxable years beginning after .December 31, 2008

children.heading: Foreign oil and gas loss defined
children.label: D Foreign oil and gas loss defined

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
I
children.text: the gross income for the taxable year from sources without the United States and its possessions (whether or not the taxpayer chooses the benefits of this subpart for such taxable year) taken into account in determining the combined foreign oil and gas income for such year, is exceeded by

children.label: II
children.text: the sum of the deductions properly apportioned or allocated thereto.

children.heading: Net operating loss deduction not taken into account
children.label: ii Net operating loss deduction not taken into account
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the net operating loss deduction allowable for the taxable year under section 172(a) shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Expropriation and casualty losses not taken into account
children.label: iii Expropriation and casualty losses not taken into account
I
children.text: any foreign expropriation loss (as defined in section 172(h) (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990)) for the taxable year, or

children.label: II
children.text: any loss for the taxable year which arises from fire, storm, shipwreck, or other casualty, or from theft,

children.heading: Foreign oil extraction loss
children.label: iv Foreign oil extraction loss
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B)(ii)(I), foreign oil extraction losses shall be determined under this paragraph as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Energy Improvement and Extension Act of 2008.

children.heading: Oil and gas extraction taxes
children.label: 5 Oil and gas extraction taxes
children.text: The term “oil and gas extraction taxes” means any income, war profits, and excess profits tax paid or accrued (or deemed to have been paid under section 960) during the taxable year with respect to foreign oil and gas extraction income (determined without regard to paragraph (4)) or loss which would be taken into account for purposes of section 901 without regard to this section.

children.heading: Disregard of certain posted prices, etc.
children.label: d Disregard of certain posted prices, etc.
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, in determining the amount of taxable income in the case of foreign oil and gas extraction income, if the oil or gas is disposed of, or is acquired other than from the government of a foreign country, at a posted price (or other pricing arrangement) which differs from the fair market value for such oil or gas, such fair market value shall be used in lieu of such posted price (or other pricing arrangement).

children.heading: Carryback and carryover of disallowed credits
children.label: f Carryback and carryover of disallowed credits

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the amount of the foreign oil and gas taxes paid or accrued during any taxable year exceeds the limitation provided by subsection (a) for such taxable year (hereinafter in this subsection referred to as the “unused credit year”), such excess shall be deemed to be foreign oil and gas taxes paid or accrued in the first preceding taxable year and in any of the first 10 succeeding taxable years, in that order and to the extent not deemed tax paid or accrued in a prior taxable year by reason of the limitation imposed by paragraph (2). Such amount deemed paid or accrued in any taxable year may be availed of only as a tax credit and not as a deduction and only if the taxpayer for such year chooses to have the benefits of this subpart as to taxes paid or accrued for that year to foreign countries or possessions.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
i
children.text: the foreign oil and gas taxes paid or accrued during such taxable year, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts of the foreign oil and gas taxes which by reason of this subsection are deemed paid or accrued in such taxable year and are attributable to taxable years preceding the unused credit year; or

children.label: i
children.text: the taxes paid or accrued (or deemed to have been paid under section 960) to all foreign countries and possessions of the United States during such taxable year,

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such taxes which were deemed paid or accrued in such taxable year under section 904(c) and which are attributable to taxable years preceding the unused credit year, plus

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount of the foreign oil and gas taxes which by reason of this subsection are deemed paid or accrued in such taxable year and are attributable to taxable years preceding the unused credit year.

children.heading: Special rules
children.label: 3 Special rules
A
children.text: In the case of any taxable year which is an unused credit year under this subsection and which is an unused credit year under section 904(c), the provisions of this subsection shall be applied before section 904(c).

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of determining the amount of taxes paid or accrued in any taxable year which may be deemed paid or accrued in a preceding or succeeding taxable year under section 904(c), any tax deemed paid or accrued in such preceding or succeeding taxable year under this subsection shall be considered to be tax paid or accrued in such preceding or succeeding taxable year.

children.heading: Transition rules for pre-2009 and 2009 disallowed credits
children.label: 4 Transition rules for pre-2009 and 2009 disallowed credits

children.heading: Pre-2009 credits
children.label: A Pre-2009 credits
children.text: In the case of any unused credit year beginning before , this subsection, as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Energy Improvement and Extension Act of 2008, shall apply to unused oil and gas extraction taxes carried from such unused credit year to a taxable year beginning after .January 1, 2009December 31, 2008

children.heading: 2009 credits
children.label: B 2009 credits
children.text: In the case of any unused credit year beginning in 2009, the amendments made to this subsection by the Energy Improvement and Extension Act of 2008 shall be treated as being in effect for any preceding year beginning before , solely for purposes of determining how much of the unused foreign oil and gas taxes for such unused credit year may be deemed paid or accrued in such preceding year.January 1, 2009
